ops.title: macOS arm64 ty (0.0.45) silently misses diagnostics its Linux build reports — a green local ty check does not predict the CI gate; verify on Linux via docker before pushing
ops.body:
Observed 2026-07-02 in cc-pushback: `uv run ty check cc_pushback` passed clean on macOS (arm64, ty 0.0.45) while CI (ubuntu, same ty 0.0.45, same uv.lock, same commit) failed with 7 genuine diagnostics (optional-narrowing, dict[str, object] iteration, None flowing into str params). Ruled out: versions, lockfile drift, user config, `--python-platform linux` (still passed locally), import resolution, stale caches, pristine clone. A Linux docker run reproduced CI exactly — the platform-specific ty binaries just disagree.

**Why:** ty ships per-platform Rust binaries; at 0.0.x the macOS build demonstrably drops whole diagnostic classes. Local green is necessary, not sufficient.

**How to apply:** before pushing anything gated by ty, run the authoritative check:
`docker run --rm -e UV_PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT=/tmp/venv -v <repo>:/repo -w /repo ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:python3.13-bookworm-slim sh -c "uv sync --extra dev -q 2>/dev/null; uv run ty check cc_pushback"`
(`UV_PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT` keeps the container from clobbering the host `.venv` with Linux binaries — a plain mount + sync does exactly that, then the host needs `uv sync --extra dev` to recover.) Re-test whether the platforms still disagree after each ty version bump; delete this memory when they converge. Related: [[fix-tooling-breakage-dont-punt]].
